methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $73M - $3.6B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $3.63B

private.name: Enovix
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $1.0B

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $72.51M

data.narrative:
Solid Power is a public-stage Climate & Energy company founded by Doug Campbell that has raised $232M to date and currently trades at a $504M valuation. The peer universe comprises 9 comparable companies across the Climate & Energy sector—1 public and 8 private—including QuantumScape, Enovix, LanzaTech, Helion, and Redwood Materials.

Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables (QuantumScape at $3.6B, Enovix at $1.0B, and LanzaTech at $73M) yields an implied valuation of $1.0B. This method is applicable and reflects the heterogeneity of the peer set, with a wide range spanning from $73M to $3.6B, indicating substantial dispersion in how the market values companies in this sector at the public stage.

The triangulated valuation range is $850M to $1.1B, with a median of $1.0B. This analysis is based on a single method, limiting its robustness; additional valuation approaches would strengthen confidence in the range. The current $504M market valuation sits notably below the peer median, suggesting either a discount to comparable companies or potential re-rating risk. Key uncertainties include technology execution risk specific to solid-state battery development, commercialization timelines, and relative competitive positioning within the broader frontier-energy investment landscape.
